skill.name: legend-shop-supply-api
skill.description: Connects Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to the LegendShop supply platform for product pool queries, product details, price and inventory checks, and developer account balance lookup.
skill.summary: Query LegendShop supply products, inventory, prices, and developer balance through the included API client.
skill.author: legendmall

security_audit.summary: Static analysis raised many pattern alerts; manual review dismisses the weak-crypto, entropy, and most documentation command examples as false positives. However, the executable client contains hardcoded API credentials, prints bearer tokens, disables proxy controls, and performs authenticated external API requests, so publication should wait for remediation.

high_findings.title: Hardcoded API Client Credentials
high_findings.description: The Python client embeds a client_id and client_secret, and the OpenAPI spec repeats the same values as defaults. Public distribution would expose credentials that can request bearer tokens and access product or account APIs if still valid.
high_findings.recommendation: Remove bundled secrets. Require users to provide their own credentials through a secure secret store or environment variables.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential names and literal values are present in executable code and API documentation. The same client is used to request bearer tokens, confirming operational impact.

medium_findings.title: Bearer Token Printed to Standard Output
medium_findings.description: The token command prints the access_token value directly. This can expose a live bearer token in assistant transcripts, logs, shell history, or copied output.
medium_findings.recommendation: Avoid printing tokens by default. Store tokens in memory for immediate API calls or redact token values in user-visible output.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path calls get_token and serializes the access_token field to stdout. Exposure requires the token command to be run, but the code path is explicit.

medium_findings.title: Proxy Controls Disabled for Outbound Requests
medium_findings.description: The script clears HTTP and HTTPS proxy variables and sets trust_env to false. This can bypass organization proxy controls, monitoring, or egress policy when the skill makes external API requests.
medium_findings.recommendation: Do not override proxy settings. Let the runtime environment decide proxy and certificate policy, or make proxy bypass an explicit user-controlled option.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code deliberately disables proxy environment settings before network calls. The behavior is clear, although it may have been intended as connectivity troubleshooting.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated External Network Calls Using Bundled Credentials
medium_findings.description: The script makes POST requests to the LegendShop API for token creation, product data, product detail, and account balance. Network access is expected for this skill, but it is risky because the requests use bundled credentials and bearer authorization.
medium_findings.recommendation: Require explicit user configuration for credentials and document all outbound endpoints before publication.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The requests are direct and target the documented API host. This is legitimate functionality, but the credential handling increases the impact.

low_findings.title: Insecure HTTP Endpoint and Preview Asset
low_findings.description: The documentation lists an HTTP test endpoint, and the preview page loads a product image over HTTP. These are not active credential exfiltration paths in the script, but they can cause mixed-content or downgrade concerns.
low_findings.recommendation: Use HTTPS endpoints and assets wherever possible. Clearly mark test-only URLs and avoid HTTP resources in marketplace previews.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The HTTP URL and image source are visible in documentation and preview files. The executable client uses HTTPS, so this is a lower-severity issue.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto and Entropy Alerts Appear to Be False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm and high-entropy alerts point to ordinary API documentation, CSS, sample data, or credential-like strings rather than cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, custom encryption, packed binaries, or obfuscated execution logic in the reviewed files.
low_findings.recommendation: Treat these static alerts as dismissed unless new obfuscated files or weak crypto code are added.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found markup, documentation text, and API metadata at representative flagged lines. The confirmed secret exposure is handled separately.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Literal in Executable Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The script stores API credential values in top-level constants and uses them for token acquisition.
dangerous_patterns.recommendation: Move credentials to user-managed secrets and rotate the exposed client secret.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The values are literal assignments and are passed directly in the token request payload.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Proxy Override
dangerous_patterns.description: The client modifies proxy-related environment variables and disables requests environment trust before outbound network calls.
dangerous_patterns.recommendation: Remove environment mutation and respect caller network policy.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code comments and assignments explicitly show proxy disabling.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive Token Output Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The token command returns a bearer token as visible command output.
dangerous_patterns.recommendation: Redact token output or remove the standalone token command for marketplace use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The printed JSON includes the access_token returned by the API.

content.value_statement: Merchants need current supply data before choosing products or checking account status. This skill provides guided API access for product lists, product details, inventory, prices, and balance lookup.
actual_capabilities: Retrieves an access token from the LegendShop token endpoint.
Queries paginated product pool identifiers from the supply platform.
Fetches product detail records by product identifier.
Checks developer account balance through the capital account endpoint.
Summarizes returned product, price, inventory, SKU, and balance fields for users.
limitations: Requires valid LegendShop API credentials and product pool permissions.
Only supports the endpoints documented in the included OpenAPI file.
Does not place orders, manage fulfillment, or modify account data.
Returned data quality depends on the external LegendShop API response.

use_cases.title: Review Product Supply Options
use_cases.description: Browse product pool results and request details before adding items to a store catalog.

use_cases.title: Check Price and Inventory Details
use_cases.description: Look up SKU, price, inventory, and product parameter data for sourcing decisions.

use_cases.title: Monitor Developer Account Balance
use_cases.description: Query the developer capital account endpoint before operational or purchasing workflows.

prompt_templates.title: List Available Products
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the first page of LegendShop supply products with product IDs and total count.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect One Product
prompt_templates.prompt: Get details for product ID <product_id>. Summarize price, inventory, SKU options, images, and key parameters.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Sourcing Candidates
prompt_templates.prompt: Query a product page, choose three candidate product IDs, then get details for each and compare sourcing signals.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Account Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the developer account balance and explain whether the API response indicates permission or funding problems.

output_examples.output: The response lists the current page, total product count, and product identifiers returned by the supply product pool.

output_examples.input: Get details for this product ID.
output_examples.output: The response summarizes the product name, supplier identifier, price, available SKU data, image references, and product parameters.
The response reports the account balance result, API status, success flag, and any permission message returned by the platform.

best_practices: Use user-owned credentials and rotate any credentials that were exposed in shared files.
Start with product list queries, then request details only for selected product IDs.
Confirm product permissions and account balance before relying on operational results.
anti_patterns: Do not publish hardcoded client secrets or shared bearer tokens.
Do not treat sample preview product data as live inventory data.
Do not bypass proxy or network policy without explicit user approval.

faq.question: What does this skill connect to?
faq.answer: It connects to the LegendShop supply platform API for product, detail, token, and balance endpoints.

faq.question: Does it need credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. It needs valid LegendShop client credentials and permissions for the requested endpoints.

faq.question: Can it place orders?
faq.answer: No. The included files only document product queries, detail lookup, token creation, and balance lookup.

faq.question: Can it show real-time inventory?
faq.answer: It can show inventory fields returned by the external API, subject to platform freshness and permissions.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish as provided?
faq.answer: No. The audit found hardcoded API credentials and token exposure that should be fixed first.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, using the included Python client and documentation.
